title: DNA extraction and Nanopore library prep
author: Bernard Y Kim
materials: Briefly spin down the tube to collect condensation.

steps.title: Place flies on a sheet of...
steps.instruction: Place flies on a sheet of filter paper and briefly dab with a Kimwipe to remove excess ethanol, then transfer the fly to a 1.5 mL LoBind tube. DNA LoBind Tubes, 1.5 mL Eppendorf Catalog #0030108051

steps.title: Add 500 µL Buffer STE to...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL Buffer STE to the tube with the fly. Hydration Buffer (STE) [400mM NaCl 20mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 30mM EDTA] Contributed by users

steps.title: Incubate on a platform rocker or...
steps.instruction: Incubate on a platform rocker or rotator for 00:15:00 at low to medium speed.

steps.title: Replace the solution with 500 µL...
steps.instruction: Replace the solution with 500 µL o of fresh Buffer STE and incubate for another 00:15:00 on the rocker at low to medium speed. Hydration Buffer (STE) [400mM NaCl 20mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0 30mM EDTA] Contributed by users

steps.title: Add 283 µL lysis buffer to...
steps.instruction: Add 283 µL lysis buffer to the tube with the fly. Lysis Buffer (LB) [0.1M Tris-HCl pH 8.0; 0.1M NaCl; 20mM EDTA] Contributed by users

steps.title: Using a pellet pestle, manually homogenize the fly tissue
steps.instruction: Using a pellet pestle, manually homogenize the fly tissue. While it's important to squish the fly enough so that soft tissue can be digested, it is okay for some bigger pieces of tissue, particularly those held together by tough cuticle like the legs, to remain. Pellet Pestle (Kimble Kontes, K749520-1590, 1.5 mL Polypropylene w/o Microtubes RNAse DNase free, https://www.fishersci.com/shop/products/kimble-chase-kontes-pellet-pestle-14/p-155789 ) TIP : Make sure fly bits aren't stuck on the pestle before discarding. Every bit of tissue counts!

steps.title: Spin down 1 2mL phase lock...
steps.instruction: Spin down 1 2mL phase lock gel tube per sample at 15000 x g, °C, for 00:00:30 . Although not essental, phase lock gel tubes help minimize shearing and loss of yield caused by repeated pipetting. Dow Corning High Vacuum Grease is compositionally identical to the light phase lock gel material. We buy the 5.3oz tube from Amazon and squeeze some into a 10mL BD syringe for dispensing. This size of tube/syringe fits well for minimal mess and hassle. Avoid overfilling and air bubbles. We autoclave but be warned this may cause a mess, so wrap the syringe in foil beforehand. About 250 µL of grease is placed into a 2mL LoBind tube to make the homebrew phase lock gel tube. IMPORTANT : If an insufficient amount of grease is applied, the phase lock layer will collapse during the chloroform extraction. Reference: https://bitesizebio.com/18944/diy-phase-separating-gel-clean-and-cheap/ WARNING : If you are using normal tubes in lieu of LoBinds, do not use polystyrene tubes for the phenol-chloroform extraction. They will melt and burst in the centrifuge. Polypropylene tubes do not melt. 5PRIME Phase Lock Gel tube, light, 2mL (Quantabio, 2302820, Light, https://www.quantabio.com/phase-lock-gel ) DNA LoBind Tubes 2.0 mL Eppendorf Catalog #30108078

steps.title: Briefly spin down the homogenate/lysis solution,...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the homogenate/lysis solution, then transfer to the phase lock gel tube by decanting or pipetting with a wide-bore tip.

steps.title: Add an equal volume (about 350...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume (about 350 µL ) of Tris-saturated phenol chloroform isoamyl alcohol (PCI) to the phase lock tube. This should be performed inside the fume hood. Phenol Chloroform Isoamyl Alcohol (25:24:1) Tris-saturated (pH 8.0) Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP1752I-400 (CAS: 108-95-2)

steps.title: Mix by placing tubes on a...
steps.instruction: Mix by placing tubes on a rocker at medium speed for 00:08:00 . We use a rocking platform, so the tubes are placed on their sides horizontally to maximize the surface area. When solution is well mixed, aqueous (top) layer will be a cloudy milky color.

steps.title: Centrifuge the phase lock tube at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the phase lock tube at 16000 x g, °C, for 00:08:00 . Phase lock layer should now separate aqueous and organic layers.

steps.title: Perform one more PCI extraction
steps.instruction: Perform one more PCI extraction: go to step #12 . Use the same tube for the entire extraction, i.e. just add another 350uL of PCI/CHCl3 to the tube and proceed. The phase lock layer should hold through the extraction steps.

steps.title: Add an equal volume (usually 350...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume (usually 350 µL ) of chloroform to the tube. This should be performed inside the fume hood. Chloroform Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #CX1055-6 (CAS: 67-66-3)

steps.title: Mix by placing tubes on a...
steps.instruction: Mix by placing tubes on a rocker at medium speed for 00:08:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge the phase lock tube at...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the phase lock tube at 16000 x g, °C, for 00:08:00 . Phase lock layer should now separate aqueous and organic layers.

steps.title: Quickly decant the aqueous (top) layer into a fresh 1
steps.instruction: Quickly decant the aqueous (top) layer into a fresh 1.5 mL LoBind tube. We find decanting more effective than pipetting. If the pipette tip touches the phase lock gel, it makes a huge mess. Try to perform the decanting step in a few seconds, and don't tap/shake the phase lock tube to get the last drops out. Care must be taken as the chloroform weakens the phase lock gel layer. If the phase lock tube is inverted for too long during decanting, the layer will collapse and everything will pour out. It's best to leave a couple of drops behind to avoid the hassle of cleaning this up. IMPORTANT : It is highly recommended to use LoBind tubes in this and subsequent steps. The coating will prevent DNA sticking to the tube. This is helpful for maximizing yield and minimizing shearing. This should be performed inside the fume hood. DNA LoBind Tubes, 1.5 mL Eppendorf Catalog #0030108051

steps.title: Chill 100% ethanol on ice and...
steps.instruction: Chill 100% ethanol on ice and make 500 µL of fresh 70% ethanol using nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.1 volume (typically 30 µL ) of 3M sodium acetate to the sample. Gently swirl to mix. 3M sodium acetate Contributed by users

steps.title: Add 2-2
steps.instruction: Add 2-2.5 volumes (typically 675 µL ) of cold 100% ethanol to the tube, and mix with careful swirling and gentle rocking. It will be difficult to see precipitated DNA, but extractions from larger flies will usually have a few visible strands. If the extraction tube turns cloudy, it is likely salt precipitation because the solution is too nonpolar. Add water dropwise with thorough mixing and the solution should clear up.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 5000 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 5000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 to pellet the DNA. TIP : the pellet will often be invisible. Centrifuge the tubes with the hinge placed consistently towards the outside of the rotor so that you know where the pellet will be.

steps.title: While being careful not to disturb...
steps.instruction: While being careful not to disturb the pellet, pipette off the ethanol.

steps.title: Add 200 µL of 70% ethanol to wash the DNA
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL of 70% ethanol to wash the DNA. Gently swirl to mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube at 5000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube at 5000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: While being careful not to disturb...
steps.instruction: While being careful not to disturb the pellet, pipette off the ethanol. I usually spin the tube down briefly (~2 sec) and use a P10/P20 to grab the last bit of ethanol in the tube.

steps.title: Wash the pellet once more
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet once more: go to step #25 .

steps.title: Allow the tube to air dry no longer than 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Allow the tube to air dry no longer than 00:01:00 . Do not over-dry .

steps.title: Resuspend in 42 µL 10mM Tris...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 42 µL 10mM Tris and incubate at 50 °C for 00:30:00 . Carefully pipette the tris up and down the back of the tube to ensure you've resuspended all the DNA. Sometimes it will be spread out a bit along the back of the tube, rather than pelleted cleanly on the bottom. Elution Buffer (EB) [10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0] Contributed by users

steps.title: Briefly spin down tube to gather...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down tube to gather any condensation and store at 4 °C .

steps.title: Pipette mix slowly 5X with a...
steps.instruction: Pipette mix slowly 5X with a P200 tip to ensure proper resuspension.

steps.title: Check sample concentration and quality of...
steps.instruction: Check sample concentration and quality of 1 µL aliquots using Qubit and Nanodrop. Ideally, you'll have recovered >250ng DNA and absorbance ratios 260/280 >1.8 and 260/230 >2.0. We have successfully prepared Nanopore libraries with as little as 208ng of gDNA at this point.

steps.title: Reserve at least 20 ng gDNA...
steps.instruction: Reserve at least 20 ng gDNA in 6 µL Tris for Illumina library prep. Store at -20 °C until ready for library prep. This is a conservative amount. As of April 2022, we use the Illumina kit with 1/5 reaction volumes to prepare libraries. We have successfully prepared Drosophila Illumina libraries with as little as 4-6 ng of gDNA depending on the kit and protocol. Your mileage may vary. Although Illumina sequencing may no longer be necessary for genome assembly with the release of new flow cell chemistries, a short-read library will be useful for variant calling if this a wild-caught individual, so this is recommended irrespective of flow cell/kit chemistry.

steps.title: Thaw NEBNext repair and dA-tailing mixes...
steps.instruction: Thaw NEBNext repair and dA-tailing mixes and buffers from the Nanopore Companion Module. Mix buffers by vortexing and enzyme mixes by flicking. Spin down tubes and keep chilled on ice. NEBNext Companion Module forOxford Nanopore Technologies Ligation Sequencing – 24 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #E7180S

steps.title: Add all non-reserved DNA (up to...
steps.instruction: Add all non-reserved DNA (up to 40 µL ) to a PCR tube. Dilute the HMW DNA with water to a final volume of 48.5 µL . Add 3.5 µL of FFPE DNA repair buffer, 3.5 µL of end-prep reaction buffer, 2 µL of FFPE DNA repair mix, and 3 µL of end-prep reaction mix to the tube. Mix tube with gentle flicking (or very gentle pipetting with a cut-off P200 tip). PCR Tubes, 0.2mL, flat cap, natural, PCR Tube; 0.2mL; Natural; w/flat cap; 1000/Pk. Thermo Fisher Catalog #3412 Nuclease-free water or water filtered using a Milli-Q filtering system Ambion Catalog #AM9932

steps.title: In a thermal cycler, incubate at...
steps.instruction: In a thermal cycler, incubate at 20 °C for 01:00:00 then 65 °C for 00:30:00 . After this, sample can be held at 4 °C temporarily until ready to proceed.

steps.title: Using a cut-off P200 tip (a...
steps.instruction: Using a cut-off P200 tip (a wide bore will be too small to fit in the PCR tube), gently transfer sample to a 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube. Add 60 µL AMPure beads. Using a wide-bore P200 tip, quickly but gently mix the tube. DNA LoBind Tubes, 1.5 mL Eppendorf Catalog #0030108051 Large-Orifice Pipet Tips 200μL Fisher Scientific Catalog #02-707-134 Agencourt AMPure XP Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63880

steps.title: Allow the sample to sit at...
steps.instruction: Allow the sample to sit at least 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow the beads to bind the DNA. Meanwhile, prepare 500 µL fresh 70% ethanol with nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Pellet the beads by placing the...
steps.instruction: Pellet the beads by placing the tube onto a magnetic tube rack for 00:05:00 or until sample has completely cleared. Magnetic 1.5 mL tube rack (Any, NA)

steps.title: Pipette off the supernatant, taking care...
steps.instruction: Pipette off the supernatant, taking care not to disturb the bead pellet.

steps.title: Add 175 µL of 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 175 µL of 70% ethanol. Pipette slowly, with the tip touching the front wall of the tube, so that the pellet is not disturbed.

steps.title: Pipette off the supernatant, taking care...
steps.instruction: Pipette off the supernatant, taking care not to disturb the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Wash the pellet once more with...
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet once more with 70% ethanol go to step #42 .

steps.title: Briefly spin the sample tube down...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin the sample tube down and place back onto the magnet. Remove any remaining drops on the bottom of the tube with a P10. Magnetic 1.5 mL tube rack (Any, NA)

steps.title: Immediately resuspend bead pellet in 31 µL nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Immediately resuspend bead pellet in 31 µL nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Incubate the tube on the heat...
steps.instruction: Incubate the tube on the heat block at 50 °C for 00:30:00 . Every 5 minutes, gently flick the tube to encourage any settled beads to resuspend.

steps.title: Briefly spin down the tube to...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the tube to collect condensation then place tube back on the magnetic rack. Allow tube to sit for 00:05:00 or until sample has completely cleared.

steps.title: With a cut-off P200 tip, transfer...
steps.instruction: With a cut-off P200 tip, transfer 31 µL eluate to a fresh 1.5 mL LoBind tube. DNA LoBind Tubes, 1.5 mL Eppendorf Catalog #0030108051

steps.title: Check for recovery with Qubit using 1 µL of sample
steps.instruction: Check for recovery with Qubit using 1 µL of sample. Recovery should be at least 150 ng DNA at this point.

steps.title: Thaw AMXII, T4 ligase, LNB, and...
steps.instruction: Thaw AMXII, T4 ligase, LNB, and LFB from the NEBNext Nanopore Companion Module and the Nanopore LSK110 kit. Mix AMXII, T4 ligase, and LFB by flicking. Mix LNB by pipetting. Briefly spin the tubes down and keep chilled on ice. NEBNext Companion Module forOxford Nanopore Technologies Ligation Sequencing – 24 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #E7180S Oxford Nanopore Ligation Sequencing Kit Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-LSK110

steps.title: Add 30 µL prepared DNA sample, 2
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL prepared DNA sample, 2.5 µL AMX, and 5 µL T4 ligase to a fresh 1.5 mL DNA LoBind tube. Gently flick the tube to mix. These are 1/2 the standard prep volumes, hence elution in 31uL in Step 47. DNA LoBind Tubes, 1.5 mL Eppendorf Catalog #0030108051 NEBNext Companion Module forOxford Nanopore Technologies Ligation Sequencing – 24 rxns New England Biolabs Catalog #E7180S Ligation sequencing kit 1D Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-LSK109

steps.title: Add 12
steps.instruction: Add 12.5 µL LNB to the sample. Working quickly, mix by gentle pipetting with a wide-bore tip. DNA precipitation is normal, but if the DNA precipitates before you finish mixing it will stick to your pipette tip and you will lose a significant amount of library. Oxford Nanopore Ligation Sequencing Kit Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-LSK110 Large-Orifice Pipet Tips 200μL Fisher Scientific Catalog #02-707-134

steps.title: Incubate the reaction mixture at Room temperature for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate the reaction mixture at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 20 µL AMPure beads and...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL AMPure beads and mix gently by flicking until uniform in color. Let sample sit for at least 00:05:00 , with occasional gentle mixing if necessary, so the DNA can bind to the beads. Agencourt AMPure XP Beckman Coulter Catalog #A63880

steps.title: Pellet the beads by placing the...
steps.instruction: Pellet the beads by placing the tube onto a magnetic tube rack for 00:05:00 or until sample has completely cleared. Magnetic 1.5 mL tube rack (Any, NA)

steps.title: Pipette off the supernatant, being careful...
steps.instruction: Pipette off the supernatant, being careful not to disturb the bead pellet.

steps.title: Remove tube from magnetic rack and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube from magnetic rack and add 100 µL of LFB to the tube, flicking to mix. Oxford Nanopore Ligation Sequencing Kit Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-LSK110 DO NOT USE ETHANOL TO WASH PREPARED LIBRARY. It will denature the motor protein.

steps.title: Pellet the beads by replacing the...
steps.instruction: Pellet the beads by replacing the tube onto the magnetic tube rack for 00:05:00 or until sample has completely cleared. Magnetic 1.5 mL tube rack (Any, NA)

steps.title: Being careful not to disturb the...
steps.instruction: Being careful not to disturb the pellet, pipette off all the supernatant.

steps.title: Remove the tube from the magnet and briefly spin down
steps.instruction: Remove the tube from the magnet and briefly spin down. Replace tube onto magnet and remove any remaining drops of supernatant with a P10.

steps.title: Wash the pellet with LFB one more time
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with LFB one more time. go to step #58

steps.title: Resuspend beads in 26 µL EB
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads in 26 µL EB. Elution Buffer (EB) [10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0] Contributed by users

steps.title: Incubate beads on the heat block...
steps.instruction: Incubate beads on the heat block at 37 °C for 00:15:00 . Every 5 minutes, gently flick the tube to resuspend any settled beads.

steps.title: Place sample tube onto magnetic rack...
steps.instruction: Place sample tube onto magnetic rack for at least 00:05:00 or until sample has cleared. Magnetic 1.5 mL tube rack (Any, NA)

steps.title: Using a cut-off P200 tip, transfer...
steps.instruction: Using a cut-off P200 tip, transfer 26 µL eluate into a fresh 1.5 mL LoBind tube. DNA LoBind Tubes, 1.5 mL Eppendorf Catalog #0030108051

steps.title: Quantify library concentration with Qubit using...
steps.instruction: Quantify library concentration with Qubit using 1 µL of the prepared library.

steps.title: Thaw 1 tube sequencing buffer SBII...
steps.instruction: Thaw 1 tube sequencing buffer SBII (SQK-LSK110), 1 tube loading solution LS (SQK-LSK110), 1 tube flush buffer FB (EXP-FLP002), and 1 tube flush tether FLT (EXP-FLP002). Mix SBII, LS, and FB by flicking. Mix FLT with a pipette. Spin down and keep reagents on ice until ready to sequence. Make sure the right versions of reagents from the ligation and/or auxiliary/expansion kits are being used. Oxford Nanopore Ligation Sequencing Kit Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-LSK110

steps.title: With a cut off P200 tip,...
steps.instruction: With a cut off P200 tip, transfer at least 100 ng of prepared library to a fresh 1.5mL LoBind tube. This should not exceed 25 µL in volume (i.e. the volume of the eluate from Step 64). These libraries tend to be pretty fragmented, with read N50s ranging from 2-10kbp. More than 100 ng library is needed for longer N50 libraries, but it often helps to have 2 library loads so that a flush & reload can be used to improve throughput. So this part is a bit of a guessing game that depends on library yield and sample quality. DNA LoBind Tubes, 1.5 mL Eppendorf Catalog #0030108051

steps.title: Add the volume of LS that...
steps.instruction: Add the volume of LS that will result in a total volume of 25 µL in the LoBind tube. For example, if 15 µL of prepared library was initially added to the tube, add 10 µL of LS for a total 25 µL library + LS. Oxford Nanopore Ligation Sequencing Kit Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-LSK110

steps.title: Add 25 µL SBII to the...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL SBII to the tube for a total volume of 50 µL . 50 uL is significantly lower than the standard preparation of 75 uL library+SQB+LS/LBII. We use this amount because 50 uL is enough to barely cover the flow cell membrane; this is more visibly apparent if using LBII instead of LS. This allows us to improve the concentration of library for very low yield samples. THIS VOLUME HAS NOT BEEN TESTED ON PROMETHION FLOW CELLS. Validation is needed. Oxford Nanopore Ligation Sequencing Kit Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #SQK-LSK110

steps.title: Follow the official instructions to prime...
steps.instruction: Follow the official instructions to prime the flow cell, then add the prepared library to the flow cell. When loading the library, be sure to use a wide-bore pipette tip. Gently pipette mix the library before loading to ensure even distribution of the library across the flow cell membrane. Large-Orifice Pipet Tips 200μL Fisher Scientific Catalog #02-707-134

steps.title: Over the course of a sequencing...
steps.instruction: Over the course of a sequencing run, pores will get clogged and become inactive. If you have enough library, we recommend to flush the flow cell at 10-14 hour intervals to make these pores available again. Flowcell Wash Kit Oxford Nanopore Technologies Catalog #EXP-WSH003

steps.title: Add 7
steps.instruction: Add 7.5 µL 20 mg/mL Proteinase K, 7.5 µL 10% SDS, and 2 µL 20 mg/mL RNAse A to the tube. Gently flick and swirl to mix. Monarch RNase A – 1 ml (2x0.5ml) New England Biolabs Catalog #T3018L Proteinase K Solution (20 mg/mL) RNA grade Thermo Scientific Catalog #25530049 10% SDS solution Contributed by users

steps.title: Incubate tube for 02:00:00 to 04:00:00 at 50 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate tube for 02:00:00 to 04:00:00 at 50 °C . Gently mix tube every 00:45:00 . The tube should go from cloudy (due to undigested tissue bits) to clear as the digestion proceeds. Due to the pestle homogenization there will usually be bigger pieces of the fly, like the wings and legs, floating around. This is fine, just pipette as much liquid as you can get for the next step.

steps.title: Use a pipette to remove as...
steps.instruction: Use a pipette to remove as much of the liquid as possible, but do not dry the fly.
